title: Hyundai Motor Group and Air Liquide Advance Global Partnership to Accelerate Hydrogen Ecosystem
content:
Hyundai Motor Group and Air Liquide have expanded their strategic partnership to accelerate the growth of the global hydrogen ecosystem. Announced at the Hydrogen Council CEO Summit in Seoul, the renewed Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) focuses on scaling hydrogen usage across Europe, the United States, and South Korea. The collaboration aims to build a comprehensive value chain covering production, storage, transportation, and utilization, with a specific emphasis on heavy-duty transport, logistics, and public transit.

As co-chairs of the Hydrogen Council, the companies reaffirmed their leadership in driving the energy transition. Building on successful milestones in Koreaâ€”such as the deployment of over 2,000 hydrogen buses and joint investments in refueling infrastructure like Hynet and Kohygenâ€”the partnership will develop key hubs to foster ecosystem growth. Future initiatives include expanding refueling networks, commercializing Fuel Cell Electric Vehicles (FCEVs) for diverse fleets, and establishing reliable low-carbon supply chains.
author: Analysts Account
